title: Challenges and Opportunities for Universal Health Coverage in South Asia: A Scoping Review
authors: Jayendra Sharma, Milena Pavlova, Wim Groot
description: Scoping review examining challenges and opportunities for UHC across South Asian countries, identifying underfunding, fragmented health systems, and inadequate insurance coverage as key barriers.

title: Advancing Universal Health Coverage in the WHO South-East Asia Region with a Focus on Human Resources for Health
authors: WHO South-East Asia Regional Office
description: Examines progress toward UHC in the WHO South-East Asia Region, highlighting the UHC service coverage index improvement from 47 in 2010 to 62 in 2021, with a focus on human resources for health.

title: Extending Universal Health Coverage to Informal Workers: A Systematic Review of Health Financing Schemes in Low- and Middle-Income Countries in Southeast Asia
authors: Nattanicha Wattanapisit, Wallaporn Srisakdakorn, Viroj Tangcharoensathien
description: Systematic review of health financing schemes implemented to extend UHC to informal workers in Southeast Asia, a region characterized by high prevalence of informal employment.

title: Moving toward Universal Health Coverage with a National Health Insurance Program: A Scoping Review of Experiences in Eleven Low- and Lower-Middle-Income Countries
authors: Katya Galactionova, Elizabeth Kalesha, Abena Asumadu-Sakyi, Fabrizio Tediosi
description: Assesses strategies adopted by low- and lower-middle-income countries with a national health insurance scheme to expand UHC, examining variations in progress across eleven countries.

title: Achieving Universal Health Coverage in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Challenges for Policy Post-Pandemic and Beyond
authors: Oyeli Ochie, Olalekan Olasehinde, Rebecca Adegboyega
description: Narrative review of 118 peer-reviewed and grey literature sources examining UHC challenges in LMICs, including weak health systems, financing gaps, and epidemiological transitions exacerbated by COVID-19.

title: Tracking Universal Health Coverage: 2023 Global Monitoring Report
authors: World Health Organization, World Bank
description: Joint WHO-World Bank global monitoring report tracking progress toward UHC, concluding the world is off track for significant progress by 2030 with persistent gaps in service coverage and financial protection.

title: Strengthening Primary Health Care Through Community Health Workers in South Asia
authors: Shyam Sundar Budhathoki, Samiksha Singh, Basnama Ayaz, Zulfiqar A Bhutta
description: Comparative analysis of community health worker programs in Bangladesh, India, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka, examining successes, challenges, and reforms for better-performing CHW programs.

title: Workload and Emerging Challenges of Community Health Workers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Mixed-Methods Systematic Review
authors: Tegbar Yigzaw, Wondwosen Shiferaw, Frehiwot Berhane, Mengistu Hailemariam
description: Systematic review summarizing community health workers' perceived workload in LMICs, finding that excessive workload affects productivity and retention of CHWs in remote and underserved areas.

title: Community Health Workers and COVID-19: Cross-Country Evidence on Their Roles, Experiences, Challenges and Adaptive Strategies
authors: Meenakshi Gautham, Kok Maryse, Kerry Scott, Shivam Gupta, Maryse C Kok
description: Cross-country study examining CHWs' contributions during COVID-19 in India, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sierra Leone, Kenya, and Ethiopia, analyzing their frontline roles, challenges, and adaptive strategies.

title: Task Shifting Healthcare Services in the Post-COVID World: A Scoping Review
authors: Emily Liang, Arjun Mitra, Sonia Abi Jaoude, Veronica Vargas
description: Scoping review of task shifting and task sharing approaches that move tasks from highly trained professionals to other providers, examining evidence and implications for health service delivery in LMICs.

title: Task Sharing with Non-Physician Health-Care Workers for Management of Blood Pressure in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Tazeen H Jafar, Greet P Gandhi, Angel CY Ang, Mente Andrew
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrating the effectiveness of task sharing with non-physician health workers for blood pressure management in LMICs, finding that nurses and pharmacists were relatively more effective.

title: The Nutrition Sensitivity of Food and Agriculture in South Asia
authors: Derek Headey, Harold Alderman
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper reviewing evidence on the nutrition sensitivity of food and agriculture across eight South Asian countries, addressing the region's high burdens of child stunting and wasting.

title: The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2023
authors: FAO, IFAD, UNICEF, WFP, WHO
description: Joint UN report providing global and regional estimates on hunger, food insecurity, and malnutrition, examining urbanization and agrifood system transformation as pathways to affordable healthy diets.

title: Regional Overview of Food Security and Nutrition 2020: Asia and the Pacific
authors: FAO, UNICEF, WFP, WHO
description: Joint UN report on food security and nutrition in Asia and the Pacific, assessing progress toward SDG 2 targets and noting the expected impact of COVID-19 disruptions on all forms of malnutrition.

title: Factors Associated with Wasting Among Children Under Five Years Old in South Asia: Implications for Action
authors: Nita Dalmiya, Harriet Torlesse, Victor Aguayo
description: Analysis of factors associated with child wasting across South Asia finding that low maternal BMI is a significant predictor and boys are more likely to be wasted than girls in Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan.

title: Revisiting Maternal and Child Undernutrition in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: Variable Progress Towards an Unfinished Agenda
authors: Robert E Black, Cesar G Victora, Susan P Walker, Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Parul Christian, Mercedes de Onis, Majid Ezzati, Sally Grantham-McGregor, Joanne Katz, Reynaldo Martorell, Ricardo Uauy
description: Lancet Series paper reviewing progress 13 years after the first Series on maternal and child undernutrition, finding that stunting prevalence has fallen but wasting and stunting remain major public health problems in low-income countries.

title: Evidence-Based Interventions for Improvement of Maternal and Child Nutrition: What Can Be Done and at What Cost?
authors: Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Jai K Das, Arjumand Rizvi, Michelle F Gaffey, Neff Walker, Susan Horton, Patrick Webb, Anna Lartey, Robert E Black
description: Lancet Nutrition Series paper providing systematic review of evidence-based nutrition interventions for maternal and child health, estimating costs and potential impact of scaling up in high-burden countries.

title: Contribution of Malnutrition to Infant and Child Deaths in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
authors: Cynthia Boschi-Pinto, Emily R Smith, Jonathon L Simon, Myo Myo Mon, Alka Dev
description: Study using postmortem data from seven LMICs revealing that malnutrition plays a significant role in the causal chain of 40% of deaths among children under 5 years of age in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.

title: Socioeconomic Inequalities in Access to Maternal Healthcare in South-Asian Countries: A Systematic Review
authors: Parveen Ali, Rubab Zahra, Shehla Naeem, Sana Waqas
description: Systematic review of 55 studies from India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, and Pakistan showing that maternal healthcare access is linked to economic status, education, and women's autonomy in patriarchal family structures.

title: Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health Intervention Coverage in 70 Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries, 2000-30: Trends, Projections, and Inequities
authors: Countdown to 2030 Collaboration
description: Lancet Global Health analysis projecting RMNCH intervention coverage across 70 LMICs through 2030, finding that only 18 countries will reach the 80% Composite Coverage Index target, with South Asia projected to reach 89.2%.

title: A Global Analysis of the Determinants of Maternal Health and Transitions in Maternal Mortality
authors: GBD 2021 Maternal Mortality Collaborators
description: Global Burden of Disease analysis examining determinants of maternal health transitions and maternal mortality trends, highlighting socioeconomic, health system, and demographic factors driving inequities.

title: Can Available Interventions End Preventable Deaths in Mothers, Newborn Babies, and Stillbirths, and at What Cost?
authors: Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Jai K Das, Rajiv Bahl, Joy E Lawn, Rehana A Salam, Vinod K Paul, M Jeeva Sankar, Hannah Blencowe, Arjumand Rizvi, Victoria B Chou, Neff Walker
description: Lancet Every Newborn Series paper modeling the effect and cost of scaling up interventions in 75 high-burden Countdown countries, finding that accelerated scale-up could prevent most newborn deaths at $1,928 per life saved.

title: Impact of mHealth Interventions on Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health from Conception to 24 Months Postpartum in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Systematic Review
authors: Anam Shahil Feroz, Naureen Akber Ali, Nadia Fareed, Sarah Saleem
description: Systematic review of mHealth technologies and their effectiveness on maternal, newborn, and child health outcomes during the first 1000 days of life in LMICs.

title: The Lancet Commission on Global Mental Health and Sustainable Development
authors: Vikram Patel, Shekhar Saxena, Crick Lund, Graham Thornicroft, Florence Baingana, Paul Bolton, Dan Chisholm, Pamela Y Collins, Janice L Cooper, Julian Eaton, Helen Herrman
description: Landmark Lancet Commission reassessing the global mental health agenda within the 2030 Sustainable Development framework, calling for closing gaps in access, quality, and prevention of mental health care worldwide.

title: COVID-19 and Mental Health in 8 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Prospective Cohort Study
authors: Tassew Woldehanna, Anja Benshaul-Tolonen, Manuela Angelucci, Sandra Favara, Marta Favara
description: Prospective cohort study of 21,162 individuals across 8 LMICs in Asia, Africa, and South America, finding that depression symptoms increased significantly (0.29 SD) in the first 4 months following COVID-19 onset.

title: COVID-19 Mental Health Impact and Responses in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: Reimagining Global Mental Health
authors: Brandon A Kohrt, Emily Haroz, Judith K Bass, Adam Brown, Gloria A Pedersen, Mark J D Jordans, Wietse A Tol
description: Lancet Psychiatry analysis of how LMICs, historically receiving a small fraction of global mental health resources, rapidly developed COVID-19 mental health and psychosocial support response plans.

title: Mental Health Policy Implementation in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Realist Review Protocol
authors: Ruth Trainor, Florence Baingana, Assegid Debebe
description: Protocol for a realist synthesis explaining how, why, for whom, and under what health system conditions certain mental health policy implementation strategies work or fail in LMICs.

title: Data Gaps in Prevalence Rates of Mental Health Conditions Around the World: A Retrospective Analysis of Nationally Representative Data
authors: Lena C Quilty, Michael H Boyle, John Cairney, Guilherme Borges
description: Lancet Global Health analysis revealing substantial gaps in mental health prevalence data, particularly among children and in low-income countries, despite increased scientific production.

title: Fragmented Health Systems in COVID-19: Rectifying the Misalignment Between Global Health Security and Universal Health Coverage
authors: Abiodun Awosusi, Irene Akua Agyepong, Ifeanyi M Nsofor, Agnes Soucat
description: Lancet paper examining how COVID-19 exposed overconcentration on global health security at the expense of sustainable UHC investments, using examples from African countries.

title: Redesigning Health Systems for Global Health Security
authors: Irene Akua Agyepong, Uche V Amazigo, Gorik Ooms, Moffat J Nyirenda, Agnes Soucat
description: Lancet Global Health paper arguing for identifying and strengthening critical characteristics of resilient health systems, emphasizing health information systems and data platforms for effective epidemic detection.

title: Global, Regional, and National Health-Care Inefficiency and Associated Factors in 201 Countries, 1995-2022
authors: GBD 2023 Health Care Spending Collaborators
description: Lancet Global Health study measuring health spending inefficiency across 201 countries, finding that expanding government health coverage and strengthening infrastructure could decrease health system inefficiency.

title: Measures to Strengthen Primary Health-Care Systems in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
authors: Shannon Barkley, Ernesto Bascolo, Hernan Montenegro, Shannon McManus
description: WHO Bulletin article identifying priorities for improving primary health care in LMICs, including community engagement and adapting PHC to evolving population needs for achieving UHC by 2030.

title: Guide Posts for Investment in Primary Health Care and Projected Resource Needs in 67 Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: A Modelling Study
authors: Karin Stenberg, Odd Hanssen, Melanie Bertram, Tessa Tan-Torres Edejer, Agnese Ruscitti, Callum Brindley
description: Lancet Global Health modelling study estimating that an additional $200-328 billion per year is required for PHC from 2020-2030, with the greatest needs in low-income countries.

title: The World Health Report 2008: Primary Health Care - Now More Than Ever
authors: World Health Organization
description: Landmark WHO report revisiting primary health care 30 years after the Alma-Ata Declaration, advocating for universal access, equity, and social justice through four interlocking sets of PHC reforms.

title: Primary Health Care on the Road to Universal Health Coverage: 2019 Global Monitoring Report
authors: World Health Organization, World Bank
description: WHO-World Bank monitoring report examining PHC service coverage indicators across income groups and regions, released ahead of the UN High-Level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage.

title: Delivering Non-Communicable Disease Services Through Primary Health Care in Selected South Asian Countries: Are Health Systems Prepared?
authors: Mehreen Riaz Hashmi, Syed Amjad Ali, Dina Balabanova, Sameen Siddiqi
description: Lancet Global Health Series paper examining NCD service delivery through PHC in South Asian countries, finding that implementation of PHC to address NCDs is currently insufficient despite rising NCD burden.

title: Self-Management of Chronic, Non-Communicable Diseases in South Asian Settings: A Systematic Mixed-Studies Review
authors: Farooq Siddiqui, Omara Dogar, Mona Kanaan, Kamran Siddiqi
description: Systematic review finding that age, education, income, knowledge, social support, self-efficacy, and affordability of medicine are key determinants of chronic NCD self-management in South Asia.

title: Leveraging Lessons from the COVID-19 Pandemic to Strengthen Low-Income and Middle-Income Country Preparedness for Future Global Health Threats
authors: Madhukar Pai, Nita Bhandari, Gagandeep Kang, Soumya Swaminathan
description: Lancet Infectious Diseases paper analyzing how the global COVID-19 response failed to fully prevent morbidity and mortality in LMICs and outlining lessons for future pandemic preparedness.

title: Pandemic Preparedness and COVID-19: An Exploratory Analysis of Infection and Fatality Rates, and Contextual Factors Associated with Preparedness in 177 Countries
authors: COVID-19 National Preparedness Collaborators
description: Large-scale Lancet analysis of pandemic preparedness across 177 countries, finding that traditional preparedness indices like the Global Health Security Index failed to predict COVID-19 pandemic outcomes.

title: Global Investments in Pandemic Preparedness and COVID-19: Development Assistance and Domestic Spending on Health Between 1990 and 2026
authors: Global Burden of Disease Health Financing Collaborator Network
description: Lancet Global Health open access study highlighting financing gaps in national health systems, especially in LMICs, that contributed to pandemic unpreparedness.

title: Financing Pandemic Prevention, Preparedness and Response: Lessons Learned and Perspectives for Future
authors: Iyiola Olatunji, Olawale Oladejo, Oyewale Tomori
description: Globalization and Health paper arguing that global health security goals and UHC will remain unachievable unless African and developing country health systems are adequately funded for public health emergency resilience.

title: Social Determinants of Health: The Role of Effective Communication in the COVID-19 Pandemic in Developing Countries
authors: John Osita Ataguba
description: Open access paper in Global Health Action arguing that effective crisis communication is among the most critical social determinants of health for reducing COVID-19 morbidity and relieving pressure on weak health systems in developing countries.

title: Social Determinants of Infectious Diseases in South Asia
authors: Bir Rawal, Rajesh Manandhar, Sameer Dixit
description: PMC open access paper examining social determinants of infectious diseases in South Asia with special reference to HIV and tuberculosis, concluding that addressing social factors is essential for sustainable intervention.

title: Social Determinants of Health, Report of a Regional Consultation, Colombo, Sri Lanka
authors: World Health Organization, Regional Office for South-East Asia
description: WHO South-East Asia regional consultation report on social determinants of health, examining health inequities driven by socioeconomic position and the double burden of disease in the region.

title: Towards Achievement of Universal Health Care in India by 2020: A Call to Action
authors: K Srinath Reddy, Vikram Patel, Prabhat Jha, Vinod K Paul, A K Shiva Kumar, Lalit Dandona
description: Landmark Lancet India Series paper arguing that sustaining India's economic trajectory requires giving the highest priority to universal health care, with a focus on equity, financial protection, and primary health care.

title: Health Care and Equity in India
authors: Vikram Patel, Rachana Parikh, Sunil Nandraj, Priya Balasubramaniam, Kavita Narayan, Vinod K Paul, A K Shiva Kumar, Mirai Chatterjee, K Srinath Reddy
description: Lancet India Series paper highlighting that more than three-quarters of health expenditure in India is out-of-pocket, with about 39 million people falling into poverty annually due to health spending.

title: Reimagining India's Health System: A Lancet Citizens' Commission
authors: K Srinath Reddy, Vikram Patel, Nachiket Mor, Srinivasan Ramani
description: Lancet Citizens' Commission laying out the path to achieving UHC in India, proposing a transformative approach that places citizen needs at the center of health system reform.

title: The Burden of Child and Maternal Malnutrition and Trends in Its Indicators in the States of India: The Global Burden of Disease Study 1990-2017
authors: India State-Level Disease Burden Initiative Malnutrition Collaborators
description: GBD study analyzing child and maternal malnutrition trends across Indian states, finding high prevalence of stunting in less-developed states and paradoxically high wasting in some more-developed states.

title: Demographic, Socioeconomic and Regional Disparities in the Coverage of Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Facilities in Four South Asian Countries
authors: Md Hasibul Islam Jitu, Mohammad Shahed Masud
description: PLOS ONE study using MICS data from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, and Pakistan to examine WASH facility coverage disparities and identify significant socioeconomic and demographic factors.

title: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH) Factors and the Incidence of Communicable Diseases in Urban Bangladesh: Evidence from Municipal Areas
authors: Md Nuruzzaman Khan, Raaj Kishore Biswas, Shuva Das
description: PLOS NTDs study finding that nearly one in four participants in urban Bangladesh reported communicable diseases, with diarrhoea most common and poor hand hygiene showing the strongest disease association.

title: Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH): The Evolution of a Global Health and Development Sector
authors: Sara de Wit, Euphrasia Luseka, David Bradley, Joe Brown, Jayant Bhagwan, Barbara Evans, Matthew C Freeman, Guy Howard, Isha Ray, Ian Ross, Sheillah Simiyu, Oliver Cumming, Clare I R Chandler
description: BMJ Global Health open access article examining the evolution of the global WASH sector, noting that universal WASH access by 2030 under SDG 6 remains a distant prospect in many countries.

title: Progress on Household Drinking Water, Sanitation and Hygiene 2000-2020: Five Years into the SDGs
authors: WHO, UNICEF
description: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme report presenting updated global and regional WASH estimates, serving as the leading source of comparable progress data for SDG 6 monitoring.

title: Essential Childhood Immunization in 43 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Analysis of Spatial Trends and Socioeconomic Inequalities in Vaccine Coverage
authors: Anna Dimitrova, Jayanta Bhattacharya, Nirmala Naidoo, Ties Boerma
description: PLOS Medicine open access analysis of spatial trends and socioeconomic inequalities in essential childhood immunization coverage across 43 LMICs, revealing persistent wealth and geographic disparities.

title: Vaccine Equity in Low and Middle Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Abimbola Olaniran, Aduragbemi Banke-Thomas, Helen Smith, Susan Tull
description: Systematic review highlighting that global DTP3 coverage stagnated at 85% since 2010, with 14 million infants missing initial doses in 2019, driven by wealth, education, gender, and geographic inequalities.

title: The 2024 Report of the Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change: Facing Record-Breaking Threats from Delayed Action
authors: Marina Romanello, Claudia di Napoli, Carole Green, Harry Kennard, Pete Lampard, Daniel Scamman, et al.
description: Annual Lancet Countdown report tracking 57 indicators on health and climate change, finding that heat exposure caused 470 billion lost labour hours globally in 2021, with losses equivalent to 5.6% of GDP in low-HDI countries.

title: Digital Health Interventions for Improving Access to Primary Care in India: A Scoping Review
authors: Smisha Agarwal, Yumna Vahanvaty, Manushi Patel, Olakunle Alonge
description: PLOS Global Public Health scoping review of digital health interventions to improve primary care access in India, examining telemedicine and mobile health approaches for vulnerable populations.

title: World Development Report 2004: Making Services Work for Poor People
authors: World Bank
description: World Bank flagship report arguing that broad improvements in welfare require putting poor people at the center of service provision, proposing frameworks for monitoring providers, amplifying voice, and strengthening incentives.

title: Monitoring Progress towards Universal Health Coverage at Country and Global Levels
authors: Margaret E Kruk, Anna D Gage, Catherine Arsenault, Keely Jordan, Hannah H Leslie, Sanam Roder-DeWan, Olusoji Adeyi, Pierre Barker, Bernadette Daelmans, Svetlana V Doubova
description: PLOS Medicine framework paper proposing methods for monitoring UHC progress at country and global levels, examining both service coverage and financial protection indicators.
